New Release · 2025 · Available on Amazon

The Amero

Digital Bridge to the Fintech Future

The monetary system that has governed North America for 40 years is showing fundamental cracks. In this groundbreaking work, Che-Sun Lee presents the definitive blueprint for how blockchain technology, XRP, and programmable digital money can unite three nations and secure the continent's economic future — on its own terms.

Drawing on decades of economic research, fintech law, and geopolitical analysis, The Amero moves beyond conspiracy theory to offer policymakers, economists, and engaged citizens a rigorously sourced, technologically grounded roadmap for the next century of North American prosperity.

★★★★★ Highly rated on Amazon
Order on Amazon →
About the Book

What This Book Is About

In 1999, economist Herbert Gunter Grubel proposed a shared North American currency — the Amero. The idea was dismissed as fringe. Today, with U.S. national debt surpassing $37 trillion, China rapidly advancing its Digital Yuan, and blockchain infrastructure finally mature enough to support a continental payment system, that vision is no longer fringe. It is urgent.

"Enter the Golden Gate Bridge of the AMERO to North America's Fintech Future"

The Amero: Digital Bridge to the Fintech Future moves beyond conspiracy theory to present a policy-literate, technologically grounded roadmap for a Digital Amero — directly challenging Prime Minister Mark Carney, President Donald Trump, and President Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo to act before the window closes.

Across 33 chapters and 75,000 words, Che-Sun Lee unpacks the economics of currency unions, decodes the XRP legal landscape, and draws on the hard lessons of the Eurozone to design a monetary institution that can actually work.

What You Will Learn

  1. Why the $5 trillion trapped in nostro/vostro accounts is the hidden cost of North American monetary fragmentation — and how XRP solves it.
  2. The full story of the Eurozone: what succeeded, what catastrophically failed in the 2010–2015 debt crisis, and the five institutional mistakes North America must avoid.
  3. How the GENIUS Act creates the legal architecture for fully-backed digital dollars — and why it is the Amero's regulatory foundation.
  4. A concrete three-phase implementation plan: coordination (2025–2028), parallel currency (2028–2030), and full integration (2030–2032).
  5. Why 2026 may be the decisive pivot year — and what political leaders in Ottawa, Washington, and Mexico City must do right now.
  6. The MBTI economic personalities of the U.S. (Commander), Canada (Logistician), and Mexico (Catalyst) — and how their strengths align in a monetary union.

Readership

Who This Book Is For

The Amero is written for anyone who wants to understand the forces reshaping the global financial system — and what North America must do to lead rather than follow. Whether you are setting policy, building fintech products, or simply paying attention to the world, this book equips you with the framework to act.

🏛️

Policymakers & Government Officials

North America's monetary future will be decided in Ottawa, Washington, and Mexico City. This book gives you the framework to lead that conversation.

📊

Economists & Financial Professionals

A rigorous, research-backed analysis of currency union economics, Eurozone lessons, and CBDC architecture — written for serious practitioners.

⚡

Fintech & Crypto Professionals

The definitive case for XRP as North America's bridge currency, with a deep dive into stablecoins, the GENIUS Act, and regulatory harmonization.

🌎

Business Leaders & Executives

Understand how a Digital Amero would reshape cross-border trade, investment, and supply chains across a continent of 500 million people.

📚

Curious Citizens & Students

If you've ever wondered why your dollars, loonies, and pesos don't work seamlessly together — this book explains what it would take to change that.

🎙️

Journalists & Researchers

33 chapters, 75,000 words, and a fully cited research foundation spanning academic economics, fintech law, and geopolitical analysis.

The Author

About Che-Sun Lee

Che-Sun Lee is a blockchain strategist, social entrepreneur, and passionate advocate for financial innovation. She advises governmental bodies, financial institutions, and businesses across North America and Asia on creating secure and compliant blockchain ecosystems.

A graduate of Toronto Metropolitan University (Bachelor of Technology), Lee has cultivated expertise at the intersection of blockchain regulation and public policy — engaging with audiences on decentralized finance, regulatory adaptation, and the potential of blockchain-based economies.

The Amero is her debut book. A series of blockchain education books for children is currently in development.

What Readers Are Saying

Early Praise for The Amero

A bold and necessary work. Lee brings genuine technological and policy depth to one of the most consequential monetary questions of our era. Required reading for anyone in fintech or public policy.

Early Reader — Blockchain & Digital Finance

The Amero does something remarkable — it makes a genuinely complex monetary proposal accessible, urgent, and visionary. The XRP chapter alone justifies the price of the book.

Beta Reader — Cross-Border Payments Sector

Lee has written the book this conversation was missing. She doesn't just describe the problem — she engineers the solution. Speculative nonfiction at its very finest.

Policy Researcher — Canada

A rare book that is simultaneously grounded in rigorous scholarship and written for the real world. The three-phase Amero timeline alone is worth the read.

Early Reader — Economic Policy & Trade

Speaking Engagements

Book Che-Sun Lee to Speak

Che-Sun Lee is available for keynotes, panel discussions, and workshops on North American monetary integration, blockchain regulation, CBDC policy, and the future of digital finance. She speaks to audiences of policymakers, financial professionals, and tech leaders across North America and Asia.

Articles & Research

Key Ideas from the Book

Economics · XRP · Policy

Why $5 Trillion in Nostro/Vostro Accounts Is North America's Hidden Problem

The inefficiency at the heart of cross-border payments — and how a Digital Amero backed by XRP infrastructure could unlock that capital permanently.

History · Eurozone · Lessons

Five Mistakes the Eurozone Made That North America Must Avoid

From the Greek debt crisis to Brexit, the hard lessons from Europe's currency experiment — and the institutional design choices that determine success or failure.

Regulation · GENIUS Act · CBDC

What the GENIUS Act Means for a Digital North American Currency

Understanding the proposed stablecoin regulatory framework — and why it provides the legal architecture that makes a Digital Amero possible for the first time.
